observations: ROUND-2 HIGH FINDING RE-VERIFIED CLOSED, BOTH DIRECTIONS: on a disposable full-repository clone at the real, current HEAD 3aed5d1, reproduced this evaluator's own two round-2 preamble-digest fixtures verbatim -- the 170-character variant ('Preflight: verdict PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL only; exceptions need sign-off.' / 'Baseline: one revision only. Audit: confidence<=evidence. Compare: deltas need a finding.') and the 157-character variant -- against the new byte-compare entry 13: BOTH correctly FAIL, each reporting 'preamble does not byte-match the pinned text' with the full expected-vs-got diff printed. Neither passes any longer.

BYTE-COMPARE GENUINELY EXACT, NOT FUZZY: two minimal single-character-class deviations were probed independently -- (a) a single trailing space appended to the pinned second line, and (b) a single word swapped in the pinned second line ('routes to' -> 'points to', preserving grammar and meaning) -- both correctly FAIL on the same byte-compare mismatch. This confirms the mechanism is a genuine list-equality byte-compare (Python string `==`), not a normalized, whitespace-trimmed, or case-insensitive match, closing any near-miss smuggling vector.

FULL PRIOR ATTACK BATTERY RE-RUN AND STILL CLOSED (six shapes, spanning all three prior rejection rounds): (A) a trailing free-prose paragraph after all four bullets -- FAILS ('found after bullets began'). (B) a fresh variant placing paraphrase prose BETWEEN two bullets (Preflight/Baseline), mirroring this evaluator's own round-2 between-bullets observation -- FAILS identically. (C) a fifth, unauthorized bullet routing to ecosystem/README.md -- FAILS ('expected exactly 4 bullets, found 5'). (D) a duplicate bullet (Preflight's path listed twice) -- FAILS identically on the same exact-cardinality assertion. (E) a reordered bullet list (Baseline before Preflight) -- FAILS ('bullet order/path mismatch'). (F) a single bullet simultaneously stretched to two physical lines with an 8-word/56-character bloated label -- FAILS on ALL THREE independent grounds at once (continuation-line violation, word-cap violation, char-cap violation), exactly as design_notes' revision-3 addendum claims. None of the six previously-fatal-to-a-defense attack shapes regressed.

HONEST PINNED DESIGN VERIFIED TO PASS: built the contract's own intended tools/SKILL.md edit (pinned preamble verbatim, 70-char line 1 + 84-char line 2, four one-line bullets in contracted order) independently from the contract's design_notes.pinned_preamble_text (not copy-pasted from the generator's own fixture) -- 15 net added lines; entry 13 reports 'SECTION 8 IS A CLOSED SET (pinned preamble byte-matched)' and PASS.

POSITIVE-CONTENT REQUIREMENT INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED SATISFIED BY THE PINNED BYTES: parsed the two pinned lines from design_notes.pinned_preamble_text directly (not trusting the contract's own prose claim) and confirmed programmatically that the combined text contains the literal substring 'ecosystem/README.md', the word 'router', and the phrase 'routes to, never reproduces' -- the round-2 missing_acceptance_coverage finding (the preamble's required positive content was previously asserted only in prose, never verified) is genuinely closed: because the preamble is now byte-pinned AND its pinned bytes happen to contain the required assertions, the same mechanism that prevents smuggling also structurally guarantees the positive content is present in any implementation that passes entry 13.

FULL 26-ENTRY SUITE + validate.sh, RE-VERIFIED AT THE REAL, CURRENT, COMMITTED HEAD 3aed5d1: built a fresh, independently-authored, fully honest 3-surface edit on a clean disposable clone and ran all 26 of contract 040's own verification commands verbatim, in array order: 26/26 exit 0 PASS, including the byte-compare entry 13. `bash tools/validate.sh` full sweep: 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'.

SCOPE / NON_GOALS / ESTIMATED_LINES / VERIFICATION-ARRAY LENGTH: diffed revision 3 against revision 2 -- 'scope.files_modify' paths unchanged (NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md). 'non_goals' count unchanged at 15 (only the 15th item's wording updated to describe exact-text pinning instead of a bounded length, as expected and self-consistent with the entry-13 rewrite). 'estimated_lines' (95) unchanged. 'verification' array length unchanged at 26 entries (only entry 13's acceptance_test/command text, and design_notes, were revised in place).

FINAL RESIDUAL, JUDGED AGAINST THE ROUND-1 STANDARD (as explicitly requested): with the preamble now fully pinned, the ONLY authorable free text anywhere in Section 8 is each bullet's own <=6-word/<=40-character label. This evaluator additionally probed a NEW, narrower variant of this residual not previously tested: because the label word-counter splits ONLY on whitespace (and periods), a label using non-whitespace delimiters (slashes, underscores, colons) counts as a single 'word' regardless of its actual semantic density -- e.g. the label 'PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL' (30 characters, 1 counted word) or 'verdict:PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL' (38 characters, 1 counted word) both PASS entry 13 unmodified, reproducing the preflight protocol's literal three-value verdict enum verbatim as a bullet label. Judged NON-BLOCKING for this final round, for reasons distinct from but consistent with the round-1 acceptance rationale: (1) unlike every one of the three previously-rejected findings (rounds 0, 1, and 2), which each reproduced actual operative RULE LOGIC (conditions, exceptions, obligations, comparative operators spanning multiple protocols), this residual reproduces only an enum's literal MEMBER NAMES -- identifier-level content structurally analogous to a file path or a schema field name, not the rule governing when/how each value applies, the operator-exception recording requirement, the blocking-condition list, or any other normative mechanic; (2) it requires a deliberate, unnatural authoring choice (writing a slash-joined pseudo-identifier as a label instead of a natural-language lifecycle-stage word) that the contract's own stated design intent (a short lifecycle-stage label such as Preflight/Baseline/Audit/Compare) would not organically produce -- unlike the round 0-2 defects, which were each reachable by a plausible, good-faith-adjacent implementation choice (a slightly more descriptive label or preamble); (3) it remains bounded by the same 40-character absolute ceiling regardless of delimiter choice, so even a maximally symbol-dense label cannot approach the cross-protocol synthesis capacity the round-2 preamble vector had (which combined content about all four protocols in one coherent 180-character block); (4) Mode B (post-code implementation review) provides a natural, cheap second line of defense here, exactly as the house precedent (.agent/qa/039-contract-review-rev2.json's own narrow final-round residual) established: Mode B can and should visually confirm the four shipped labels are genuinely short lifecycle-stage words, not symbol-joined vocabulary reproductions, against design_notes' explicit intent. RECOMMENDATION (optional, non-blocking, cost-free hardening for a future feature or a Mode B checklist item, not required for this contract's approval): fix the word-counter to split on a broader delimiter class (e.g. `re.split(r'[\s/_:.-]+', label)` instead of `.replace('.',' ').split()`), which would close this specific loophole at zero cost to the honest design (whose real labels contain no such delimiters).

PROCESS NOTE: this is contract 040's revision-3 (final authorized) round per the orchestrator's circuit-breaker adjudication recorded in .agent/run_state.json, which explicitly preserved this evaluator's round-2 process flag and ruled attempt 3 as the architectural-review strategy authorized under the 3-strike table, with a hard commitment that any further gate rejection triggers BLOCKED + docs/planning/DEBT.md + human escalation with no attempt 4. Given the round-2 HIGH finding and its paired missing_acceptance_coverage finding are both now genuinely and independently re-verified closed (not merely trusted from the generator's or Validator Mode A's narrative), and the one new, narrower residual found this round is judged non-blocking under an explicit, evidenced rationale, this review's final verdict is APPROVED.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: all probes in this round-4 review ran against disposable `cp -a` full-repository clones under the session scratchpad directory, outside the real repository tree, plus read-only `git log`/`git status` inspection of the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework. Nothing under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any .agent path other than this review's own artifact (.agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev3.json) was created, edited, or deleted in the real repository.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 3, fit for implementation as-is. This evaluator's round-2 HIGH finding (the closed-set fix's own bounded preamble was a length-only free-text canvas admitting a cross-protocol digest that reproduced literal verdict-enum vocabulary verbatim) is genuinely and robustly closed by the architectural fix: the preamble is now pinned byte-for-byte to an exact two-line string, verified by list equality rather than any length or word bound. Both of this evaluator's own round-2 fixtures (170-character and 157-character digests) were reproduced verbatim against the new entry 13 and both correctly fail; two additional subtle-deviation probes (a single trailing space; a single word swapped while preserving meaning and grammar) both correctly fail, confirming the mechanism is a genuine, non-fuzzy byte-compare. The paired missing_acceptance_coverage finding (the preamble's required positive content was previously unverified) is also closed: the pinned bytes were independently parsed and confirmed to literally contain 'ecosystem/README.md', the word 'router', and the 'routes to, never reproduces' assertion, so the same byte-compare mechanism structurally guarantees the required content is present in any passing implementation. The full prior attack battery from all three earlier rounds (trailing paragraph, fresh between-bullets interleave, unauthorized fifth bullet, duplicate bullet, reordered bullets, and a combined stretched-bullet-plus-bloated-label probe) was re-run in full and remains closed on every ground. The honest, independently-authored pinned design passes entry 13 and all 26 verification entries, and the full 11-check validate.sh sweep passes cleanly, all re-verified at the real, current, committed HEAD 3aed5d1. Scope, non_goals (15 items, only intentional wording updated), estimated_lines (95), and the verification array's length (26 entries) are otherwise unchanged from revision 2, confirming no unauthorized scope drift across this final revision. One new, narrower residual was found and judged non-blocking: because the bullet label word-counter splits only on whitespace, a slash- or colon-joined pseudo-identifier (e.g. 'PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL', 30 characters, counted as a single word) can reproduce a protocol's literal enum member names verbatim as a label while still passing the 6-word/40-character cap. This is judged materially narrower and non-blocking, distinct in kind from every previously-rejected finding: it reproduces only enum member names (identifier-level content), not operative rule logic (conditions, exceptions, obligations, or cross-protocol synthesis); it requires a deliberate, unnatural authoring choice the contract's own stated design intent would not organically produce; it remains bounded by the same 40-character ceiling with no cross-protocol combination capacity; and Mode B provides a natural, cheap second line of defense, mirroring the house precedent set by .agent/qa/039-contract-review-rev2.json's own narrow final-round residual. A cost-free, optional hardening recommendation (broaden the word-counter's delimiter class) is recorded for awareness, not required for approval. Fit for implementation as-is.
